mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW Z4 M40i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW Z4 M40i engine (B58B30) typically costs £5,500–£8,500 supply only, varying by generation: 2019–2022 £5,000–£8,500, 2023+ £5,500–£9,000. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,700. A water pump replacement costs £400–£1,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW Z4 M40i reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most robust modern engines. It's a significant step forward from the N55. The closed-deck design and front-mounted timing chain eliminated the N55's major weaknesses. Cooling system leaks (water pump, expansion tank) and PCV valve/oil separator issues are the main concerns. Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) are essential. The 2023+ 48V MHEV adds complexity but has proven reliable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW Z4 M40i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system leaks — water pump and expansion tank failure (£400–£1,000), PCV valve/oil separator failure (whistling noise, oil consumption, £400–£800), and oil filter housing gasket leaks (£500–£1,200). The B58 is otherwise exceptionally robust. 2023+ models may have 48V MHEV system issues (£800–£2,500).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW Z4 M40i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Z4 M40i values range from £25,000–£55,000 depending on year, condition and mileage. A £5,500–£8,500 reconditioned engine represents 10–35% of vehicle value — well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold. For most examples, replacement is financially viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW Z4 M40i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M40i uses the B58B30 engine code — BMW's 3.0-litre turbocharged inline-six. 2019–2022 models produce 340bhp; 2023+ models produce 382bhp with 48V mild-hybrid. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the cooling system leak, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — water pump and expansion tank replacement (£400–£1,000) is a straightforward repair on the B58. The water pump is known to fail with age. Replacing both the water pump and expansion tank together is recommended — they degrade at similar rates. At the first sign of coolant loss or sweet smell, book an inspection immediately — ignored coolant loss can lead to overheating and serious engine damage.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW Z4 M40i Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current G29 M40i B58 generation — the engine is consistent across both pre-2023 and 2023+ models (with minor tuning differences). All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Z4 M40i engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £75–£105/hr (independent BMW specialist) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The B58's longitudinal mount makes it relatively accessible.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW Z4 M40i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Worth it on a vehicle worth £30,000+
Replacement often £5,500–£8,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW Z4 M40i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles)
* Cooling system history — water pump and expansion tank checked
* No whistling from the engine (PCV valve) and no coolant loss
* Post-2023 model if you want the extra power and 48V mild-hybrid
* Toyota Supra/BMW M340i shared platform — parts availability is excellent
avoidIf: * Any evidence of coolant loss or overheating (cooling system risk)
* Whistling noise from the engine (PCV valve failure)
* Poor service history — the B58 is robust but needs proper oil
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Z4 M40i is a modern classic — a front-engined, rear-driven roadster with BMW's excellent B58 inline-six. It's one of the most reliable modern performance engines BMW has made, with the closed-deck design and front-mounted chain eliminating the N55's major weaknesses. Cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity — a £400–£1,000 water pump replacement is small change on a car worth £30,000–£50,000. Buy on history, maintain the cooling system, and the M40i will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare BMW Z4 M40i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW Z4 M40i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle; the B58 should start smoothly with a distinctive six-cylinder note
* Service History — 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes are essential for the B58
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — water pump and expansion tank are key risks
* PCV Valve — Any whistling from the engine? Increased oil consumption?
* Oil Filter Housing — Any oil leaks at the front of the engine?
* 48V System (2023+) — Any battery warnings? MHEV system checked?
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any hesitation or lag?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
